Contact Rare & Manuscript Collections

Postcards of female and male impersonators and cross dressing, 1900-1931, 1955
Collection Number: 7778

Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University Library

DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY

Title:
Postcards of female and male impersonators and cross dressing, 1900-1931, 1955
Repository:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Collection Number:
7778
Abstract:
Postcards portraying cross-dressing and female and male impersonators in France, Sweden, Austria, Spain, Belgium, the United Kingdom, the United States, Germany, Italy, and Romania. Postcards include photographic and photomechanical prints, some hand-colored. All are printed between circa 1900 and 1930 and depict performers on amateur, café-concert, music-hall, and legitimate stages at the high-point of cross-dressing as a form of entertainment. Many depict gallant visual narratives of women being wooed by other women dressed as men. Additional postcards include humorous images, popular genre narratives, promotional portraits of theatrical and music-hall male and female impersonators, as well as World War I POW camp theatrical productions. Two postcards are credited to female photographers, Ilse Magenmiller (German) and Jessie Westbrook (American). Sets of postcards depict narrative sequences, including Joan of Arc, Les Cerises, Chasé-croisé, Idylle à Trianon, and Une Amourette Louis XV. One such sequence, Pierrot et Columbine (circa 1900), portrays a sexual ending with Columbine represented nude (in a pink body suit). Male and female impersonators featured include Robert Bertin, Jeanne Bloch, Leopoldo Fregoli, Monte Verdi, Louis Vernassier, Sarah Bernhardt, Gaby Deslys, John Graffton, Betti Kuhn, Hetty King, Zena Dare, Vesta Tilley, Winifred Ward, and Huntley Wright, among others.
Creator:
Koskovich, Gerard
Quanitities:
380 items.
Language:
Collection material in English, French
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION

Postcards portraying cross-dressing and female and male impersonators in France, Sweden, Austria, Spain, Belgium, the United Kingdom, the United States, Germany, Italy, and Romania. Postcards include photographic and photomechanical prints, some hand-colored. All are printed between circa 1900 and 1930 and depict performers on amateur, café-concert, music-hall, and legitimate stages at the high-point of cross-dressing as a form of entertainment. Many depict gallant visual narratives of women being wooed by other women dressed as men. Additional postcards include humorous images, popular genre narratives, promotional portraits of theatrical and music-hall male and female impersonators, as well as World War I POW camp theatrical productions. Two postcards are credited to female photographers, Ilse Magenmiller (German) and Jessie Westbrook (American). Sets of postcards depict narrative sequences, including Joan of Arc, Les Cerises, Chasé-croisé, Idylle à Trianon, and Une Amourette Louis XV. One such sequence, Pierrot et Columbine (circa 1900), portrays a sexual ending with Columbine represented nude (in a pink body suit). Male and female impersonators featured include Robert Bertin, Jeanne Bloch, Leopoldo Fregoli, Monte Verdi, Louis Vernassier, Sarah Bernhardt, Gaby Deslys, John Graffton, Betti Kuhn, Hetty King, Zena Dare, Vesta Tilley, Winifred Ward, and Huntley Wright, among others.
NOTES

Collecting Program: Human Sexuality Collection.
"Female impersonator" means the individual doing the impersonating is male and "male impersonator" means the individual doing the impersonating is female.
INFORMATION FOR USERS
Cite As:

Postcards of female and male impersonators and cross-dressing, #7778. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
This collection has been digitized in its entirety and can be viewed in the Digital Collections Portal

Scope and Contents
A comic actress who played both female and male roles in music-hall revues; dressed as a portly, gray-haired, bearded gentleman looking like then President of the French Republic, Armand Fallières. Card No. 308.
Paris, France

M. Defreyn (Sarah Yacco)

M. Defreyn (Sarah Yacco) [Delivery]

Digital
M. Defreyn (Sarah Yacco) (verso) [Delivery]
Content is available online here: https://digital.library.cornell.edu/catalog/ss:24415915
Scope and Contents
An operetta star who later developed a recording and film career. In the postcard offered here, Defreyn imitates Sadda Yacco (Kawakami Sadayakko; 1871-1946), a Japanese kabuki artist who performed to audiences in Paris at the turn of the 20th century.

Fregoli nella Mimi

Digital
The digital preservation copy may be made available on request if a delivery copy is not already online.
Digital
Fregoli nella Mimi [Delivery]
Content is available online here: https://digital.library.cornell.edu/catalog/ss:24415928
Digital
Fregoli nella Mimi (verso) [Delivery]
Content is available online here: https://digital.library.cornell.edu/catalog/ss:24415929
Scope and Contents
An internationally renowned Italian quick-change artist, Fregoli both sang and acted 20 or more characters in each of his shows. He made his first splash on the Parisian stage in 1900 and subsequently presented shows in the French capital that ran for up to a year. This card presents vignettes showing poses and costumes from Fregoli's show Mimi.
Milan, Italy
Female impersonators
Collotypes (prints)
Fregoli, Leopoldo, 1867-1936

Scope and Contents
Based on the indication of the roles noted on the verso, the actors are appearing in a play based on the Charles Dickens novella The Cricket on the Hearth. On verso: Logo of a bridled horse head framed in a horse-­shoe. Pale blue rubber-stamp impression on verso giving name and city of publisher.

Scope and Contents
Inscription: Written in black ink: Mes remerciements. Black rubber-stamp impression of signature: Vernassier L. Paired portaits of Vernassier in men's clothing and in costume as grand dame. Rubber stamp on verso: "Collection J.F.M. Inscribed.

Scope and Contents
Louis Vernassier had a long and prolific career as a music-hall female impersonator whose act involved playing numerous musical instruments (this postcard lists 12 different ones, plus "tous les instruments excentriques").
Angers, France
Female impersonators
Collotypes (prints)
Vernassier, Louis

L'Aiglon - Mme Sarah Bernhardt - Scene de Wagram
95
 1902
14.1 x 9.1 cm
Undivided verso.

L'Aiglon - Mme Sarah Bernhardt - Scene de Wagram [Delivery]
Content is available online here: https://digital.library.cornell.edu/catalog/ss:24416068
Digital
L'Aiglon - Mme Sarah Bernhardt - Scene de Wagram (verso) [Delivery]
Content is available online here: https://digital.library.cornell.edu/catalog/ss:24416069
Scope and Contents
Inscription: Addressed to Mlle Marguerite Beaujean. Black-and-white reproduction of a watercolor illustration signed Espinasse. Sarah Bernhardt appearing in one of her most celebrated trouser roles as the son of Napoleon in La Aiglon (1900) by playwright Edmond Rostand, who wrote the role for her.
Paris, France
Male impersonators
Collotypes (prints)
Bernhardt, Sarah, 1844-1923
Box 1 Folder 11
Jeanne Bloch, dans Armand' retour de Londres
96
 1910
14.6 x 9 cm
Divided verso.
Digital

Digital
Jeanne Bloch, dans Armand' retour de Londres [Delivery]
Content is available online here: https://digital.library.cornell.edu/catalog/ss:24416070
Digital
Jeanne Bloch, dans Armand' retour de Londres (verso) [Delivery]
Content is available online here: https://digital.library.cornell.edu/catalog/ss:24416071
Scope and Contents
Comic actor and singer Jeanne Bloch dressed as a portly, bearded man wearing a top hat and carrying a cane. Inscription at upper left on the recto indicates that Bloch was appearing at La Scala, a music hall in Paris open from 1874 to 1936.
Male impersonators
Relief halftones (prints)
Bloch, Jeanne

Odeon - Clara Faurens

 1905

Digital
Odeon - Clara Faurens (verso) [Delivery]
Content is available online here: https://digital.library.cornell.edu/catalog/ss:24416073
Scope and Contents
Stage and silent-film actor Clara Faurens appearing at the Theatre de la'Odeon (Paris) in the role of Pierrot, the commedia della arte stock figure of the lovesick clown. Inscribed verso: Brief note to Jeanne Gorno signed E. Hacquet.
Male impersonators
Relief halftones (prints)
Faurens, Clara
Box 1 Folder 11
Chavarny
98
 1904
14.3 x 9.2 cm
Applied color
Digital
The digital preservation copy may be made available on request if a delivery copy is not already online.
Digital
Chavarny [Delivery]
Content is available online here: https://digital.library.cornell.edu/catalog/ss:24416074
Digital
Chavarny (verso) [Delivery]
Content is available online here: https://digital.library.cornell.edu/catalog/ss:24416075
Scope and Contents
Performer dressed as an 18th-century male aristocrat in a beribboned outfit and an enormous feathered cavalier hat. Inscription at upper left on the photo indicates that Mlle. Chavarny appeared at La Scala, a music hall in the 10th arrondissement of Paris open from 1874 to 1936 where many of the grand stars of the era appeared. Addressed to Isabelle Garon; signature of sender illegible.
Male impersonators
Gelatin silver prints.
Chavarney

Inscription: "Addressed to Clara (?) Boissier. inscription at upper right on the recto indicates that Mlle. Declos and Mlle. Gaity appeared at La Cigale, a music hall on the Place Pigalle in Paris that opened in 1887.

Scope and Contents
Addressed to Rose Bounias; brief message on recto signed P. Paul. Actress dressed as a 17th-century cavalier, likely in the role of one of the brothers in a production of Jacques Offenbach's opera-bouffe Barbe-Bleue (1866).
Paris, France

Scope and Contents
Music-hall protean performer Emile Grandsart, whose act involved costume quick-changes into both female and male characters.

Scope and Contents
Studio shot of Minouche, one of the stars of Madame Arthur, a leading female-impersonation cabaret of Paris in the 1950s and 1960s. In this photograph, Minouche, who was white, impersonates a black performer in the style of Eartha Kitt.
Female impersonators
Gelatin silver prints.
Minouche

Scope and Contents
Set of two postcards portraying the De Tender sisters, both music-hall performers. Alice de Tender appeared at the Folies-Bergere, the Eldorado and the Moulin Rouge; she also appeared in silent films, including the feature La Veuve Joyeuse (1913) and a number of shorts. "La Matchiche"was a popular song debuted in a music-hall show in 1902; the painter Kees van Dongen borrowed the title for a 1904 oil representing dancers at the bal populaire Le Moulin de la Galette (collection of the Musee d'Art Moderne de Troyes).
Male impersonators
Gelatin silver prints.
Tender, Fanny de
Tender, Alice de

Scope and Contents
Full set of 10 cards with photo narrative of an early 19th-century seduction scene. Postcards numbered 1-10, each with a one- to two-sentence narrative.
Processing Information
Originally mounted in a trifold cardstock holder imprinted with an art nouveau foliage pattern. The cards were removable, held in corner slots. Cards are currently housed in individual sleeves.
Paris, France
Male impersonators
Gelatin silver prints.

1er Avril (verso) [Delivery]
Content is available online here: https://digital.library.cornell.edu/catalog/ss:24416089
Scope and Contents
Series of 2 April Fool's Day ("poisson d'avril") postcards. Two women dressed in 18th-century aristocratic garb, one as a man, one as woman: In one card (untinted), both hold a basket filled with flowers and fish. Divided verso. Stamp and postmark on verso. A doggerel verse printed on the recto of each refers to the pair in the photo as "ces marquis gracieux."
Paris, France
Male impersonators
Gelatin silver prints.
Box 1 Folder 12
1er Avril
106
 1905
14 x 9.2 cm
Applied color. Undivided verso.

Scope and Contents
Series of 5 postcards number I through V showing an 18th century picnic. The series is highly unusual as the only postcards of the genre documented to date that represent a cross-dressed woman and a man dressed as a man both portraying male roles. Inscription: "Addressed to Mesdemoiselles Bonnet; signed on recto of each card: Henriette."
Male impersonators
Gelatin silver prints.

Aristocrat and a country girl
112-117
 1900-1903
creator: Stebbing
creator: Kunzli Freres Editeurs
14.2 x 9 cm
Applied color. Divided verso. Inscribed.

Scope and Contents
Captioned: Le Droit de passage, L'Invitation, Le Beau raisin, Le Goiter, and La Declaration. Same series depicted in cards 329-330.
Male impersonators
Gelatin silver prints.
Box 1 Folder 13
L'invitation
113, 115-117
 1903
creator: Stebbing
creator: Kunzli Freres Editeurs
14.2 x 9 cm
Applied color. Divided verso.
Scope and Contents
Set of four cards with photo narrative of an 18th-century seduction scene. Male impersonator as aristocrat woos a female country girl encountered on a footbridge. The captions recount the tale: "Le Droit de passage," "L'Invitation," "Le Beau raisin," "Le Goûter," "La Déclaration." Postage stamp and postmark on recto of each card. Divided verso; notice printed full width indicating "Carte Postale. Tous le pays étrangers n'acceptent pas la correspondence au recto (se renseigner à la poste). Inscription: "Series of love notes addressed to a woman named Albertine Frémoul from a man identifying himself as "votre fiancé" (signature partially illegible: "G. Rammeno..."
Male impersonators
Gelatin silver prints.
Box 1 Folder 13
L'invitation

creator: Stebbing
creator: Kunzli Freres Editeurs
14.2 x 9 cm

Paris, France

Scope and Contents
Series of three postcards of a woman dressed in late 18th-century officer's wear reacting to declarations from a woman in a dress and flowered hat. The woman is reciting the traditional French equivalent of 'he loves me, he loves me not' while plucking petals from a flower. Cards numbered I, II and III. Each postcard after the first has a rhyming couplet as a caption on the recto.
Male impersonators
Gelatin silver prints.

[Egg] (verso) [Delivery]
Content is available online here: https://digital.library.cornell.edu/catalog/ss:24416169
Scope and Contents
Complete series of six postcards showing an enormous egg in front of a painted backdrop of a bucolic landscape. Over the series, the egg opens into left and right halves to reveal a woman dressed as an 18th-century male aristocrat who pays court to a woman dressed as a female aristocrat. Postmarks show the cards were mailed successively during the course of two months, with a message on the final one in the photo-narrative stating, "Thanks, this is the last one." The dates and inscription suggest how such series were sometimes used to sustain interest in a friendly correspondence over time. Postage stamp and postmark on one corner on verso of each card. Curved impression to corners of cards indicating the set was once place in a die-cut postcard holder. Inscription: "Addressed to Mademoiselle A. Lefort; signed on recto of each card: five signed G.M.; one signed Germaine. Brief message on lower right corner of recto of each card, two in English, the rest in French. Message on last mailed card indicates "Merci, c'est la dernière."
Male impersonators
Collotypes (prints)
Box 1 Folder 15
Amourette Louis XV
146-150
 1905
14 x 9 cm
Applied color

Scope and Contents
Set of five postcards with a photo narrative of an 18th-century seduction scene involving a marquis and marquise. Each card includes a brief caption on near lower margin of the recto with a line of dialog. The first scene shows the female aristocrat seated by herself reading a book; the male aristocrat arrives and ultimately persuades her in the final scene. Same series depicted in cards 67-68 and 310.
Male impersonators
Gelatin silver prints.

One postcard (no. 3) from a series of unknown length representing a woman dressed as a male officer in a braided uniform. The officer kisses the hand of a seated woman wearing a braided dress designed to echo the uniform.
Nancy, France

Scope and Contents
Studio photo of a garconne, the French new young women of the 1920s who appropriated a variety of masculine privileges. Includes personal message about health issues, life troubles, hopes to see friend in the coming year."
Gelatin silver prints.
Box 1 Folder 16
Bonne Annee
156
 1907
14.2 x 9.3 cm
Applied color. Divided verso.

Scope and Contents
Woman dressed as a mailman in front of a studio backdrop of a village gate. Divided verso. Publisher given as E.N., card number as 8179. Personal message addressed to "Chère amie" (Félice Pinty) signed Marguerite." Inscription: "New year's greetings to "Ma chère Henriette" at Castle Hall School in Northampton, England; signed "Ton amie qui t'embrasse, Jeanne Lambert"
Gelatin silver prints.

Scope and Contents
Female impersonator wearing a wig, pearls and a flowered tutu posing as a ballerina with arms in fifth position. The card is a spoof of the celebrated dancer and demimondaine Cléo de Mérode (1875-1966). A doggerel verse printed in red on the recto refers to the dancer's charms as designed to "captiver un prince."
Female impersonator
Collotypes (prints)

Digital
Mademoiselle, ecoutez-moi donc! (verso) [Delivery]
Content is available online here: https://digital.library.cornell.edu/catalog/ss:24416211
Scope and Contents
Complete series of six postcards showing a amle impersonator approaching a young woman and convincing her to learn how to ride the young man's bicycle. The narrative takes place in front of a painted studio backdrop of a Paris city scene. Each card includes a snippet of dialog between the two characters; the woman identifies herself in one as an "ouvrière dans une grande maison de Modes de la Rue de la Paix." Divided verso; imprinted "a utiliser seulement dans le régime intérieur (FRANCE, ALGERIE et TUNISIE)."
Male impersonators
Collotypes (prints)
Box 1 Folder 17
La bretagne pittoresque - la plus belle fille de Pont-Aven
167
 1915
creator: A. Waron
13.9 x 9 cm

Scope and Contents
Female impersonator with gray hair and handlebar moustache dressed in the traditional garb of a woman from Brittany, including a bigouden (starched lace headdress), and with a market basket on one arm and an umbrella under the other. Addressed to Monsieur Dumont; signed Horace (?). Message refers teasingly to representation of gender on postcard.
Saint-Brieuc, France
Female impersonator

Content is available online here: https://digital.library.cornell.edu/catalog/ss:24416220
Digital
[Les deux gosses] (verso) [Delivery]
Content is available online here: https://digital.library.cornell.edu/catalog/ss:24416221
Scope and Contents
Three postcards (nos. 2, 3 and 4) from a series of unknown length recounting episodes adapted from the melodramatic novel Les Deux gosses (Jules Rouff et Cie., 1880) by the popular author and playwright Pierre Decourcelle (1856-1926). The "two kids" of the title are Fanfan, the son of a diplomat carted off and raised by a married pair of petty criminals, and Claudinet, the ragged natural son of the couple. Decourcelle subsequently adapted the novel as a drama; in an 1896 production at the Theatre de l'Ambigu in Paris, the characters of the two boys were cast as trouser roles for young actresses, with Fanfan played by Mlle. Melo and Claudinet by Mlle. Reye. In the postcard series, the models for the two boys likewise are male impersonators.
Male impersonators
Gelatin silver prints.

Scope and Contents
Series of two postcards (with card 174) representing scenes and characters from La Mérine à Nastasie (1902), a play by Yann Saint- Acer, written in the Saintongeais dialect of the west coast of France.

Scope and Contents
Addressed to Auguste Morel. Woman dressed as a chef wearing a toque, men's coat and men's 18th-century kneepants and hose while holding up a fluted cake pan. She leans against a decor suggesting a country kitchen. Printed in red at the bottom of the recto is a recipe for Swiss cake.
Nancy, France

Scope and Contents
Series of five comic postcards depicting a male impersonator as a woman of Marseilles. Each card has a humorous caption giving a line of dialog. Three cards (numbered 370, 373 and 374), show a fishwife carrying a scale for weighing her goods.

Scope and Contents
Novelty card montage of three photos of the same woman dressed in top hat, tails and knee britches gesturing to a rabbit sitting next to her on a bench. May represent a musical hall trained animal act. Message on verso addressed to "Mon cher Pierre" and signed "Jeanne."

Scope and Contents
Postcard of four women: three dressed as 17th-century musketeers and one dressed as a male gardener wearing a work apron and heavy clogs and holding a rake. The two women at the center are the same one who appear in the "Couple in Fancy Dress" postcard, and they are standing in the same garden.
Male impersonators
Gelatin silver prints.

Content is available online here: https://digital.library.cornell.edu/catalog/ss:24416257
Scope and Contents
Postcard showing six women posing in front of the entry to a brick building. Three are dressed in the simple outfits of working women, including one wearing a long white apron; the other three are male impersonators dressed as a gendarme in uniform; a judge in robe, collar and chapeau; and one in a frock coat and fedora.
Male impersonators
Gelatin silver prints.

[Posing with a touring car] (verso) [Delivery]
Content is available online here: https://digital.library.cornell.edu/catalog/ss:24416259
Scope and Contents
Postcard showing nine men posing in front of an open touring car on an estate with trees and buildings in the background. The man at the center is dressed as a grand society woman in a travel outfit including a heavy coat, flowered hat, fur muff and pocketbook. The men surrounding her are dressed in various costumes, including one likely as a chauffer, one as a butler with feather duster, one with a crêpe-de-chine false beard, and others in worker's coats and caps.
Female impersonators
Gelatin silver prints.

Box 1 Folder 20
Historical Illustrations: Joan of Arc
Joan, of Arc, Saint, 1412-1431--Portraits
Box 1 Folder 20
Arrive a Chinon
194
 1905
creator: Walery
14 x 9 cm
Applied color
Digital
The digital preservation copy may be made available on request if a delivery copy is not already online.
Digital
Arrive a Chinon [Delivery]
Content is available online here: https://digital.library.cornell.edu/catalog/ss:24416266
Digital
Arrive a Chinon (verso) [Delivery]
Content is available online here: https://digital.library.cornell.edu/catalog/ss:24416267
Scope and Contents
Historical scene showing Joan of Arc telling the dauphin of France that he will be crowned king at Reims. As is conventional for iconography of Joan during her period of combat, she wears men's clothing in this case, an elaborate doublet and riding boots. Conversely, this image steps outside the convention by including a halberd-bearer accompanying Joan; the footsoldier is played by a male impersonator. Inscription: "Addressed to Lucien Renault; signed "Ta soeurette [illegible]".
Paris, France
Male impersonators
Gelatin silver prints.
Box 1 Folder 20
Jeanne d'Arc

creator: A. Bergeret et Cie.

Scope and Contents
Joan of Arc in a doublet, breastplate and helmet, holding a sword and battle standard against a smoky background. A doggerel verse printed in red at upper left on the recto recounts her victory over the English. Inscription: "Addressed to Jeanne Carlin. Brief unsigned message on recto."
Nancy, France

Jeanne d'Arc brulee vive a Rouen [Delivery]
Content is available online here: https://digital.library.cornell.edu/catalog/ss:24416286
Digital
Jeanne d'Arc brulee vive a Rouen (verso) [Delivery]
Content is available online here: https://digital.library.cornell.edu/catalog/ss:24416287
Scope and Contents
Complete series of eight postcards portraying key moments in the history of Joan of Arc. Each card has a caption indicating Joan's presence at a particular place, along with a date. The first postcard shows her dressed as a simple shepherd girl hearing mystical voices; the next five show her as the savior of France dressed in full armor, with a sword and battle standard, but with the long hair of a woman; the seventh shows her "devant ses juges" wearing a simple man's doublet; and the final card shows her forcibly returned to women's clothing, wearing a white gown while tied to the stake and licked by flames.
Gelatin silver prints.
Box 2 Folder 25
Jeanne d'Arc, victorieuse des Anglais dilvre Orleans
